description: Copy src to dst. Most common instruction.
notes: Size suffix required unless operand sizes are unambiguous. AT&T: source first.

description: Move with sign-extension. Extends narrower src into wider dst.
Move with zero-extension. Fills upper bits with zero.
notes: No movzlq — movl to 32-bit reg implicitly zeros upper 32 bits of 64-bit reg.

description: Decrement %rsp by operand size, then store src at (%rsp).
notes: Default 64-bit on x86-64. pushb not encodable.

description: Load value from (%rsp) into dst, then increment %rsp.
Load Effective Address. Computes address of mem operand, stores in dst.
notes: Does NOT dereference. Useful for address arithmetic and fast multiply.

flags: OF SF ZF AF CF PF
OF SF ZF AF CF PF

description: Unsigned multiply. Result = %[r/e/]ax * src. High half stored in %[r/e/]dx.
Signed multiply. One- two- or three-operand form.
notes: One-operand: result in rdx:rax. Two-operand: dst *= src. Three-operand: dst = src * imm.

description: Unsigned divide rdx:rax by src. Quotient → rax, remainder → rdx.
Signed divide rdx:rax by src. Quotient → rax, remainder → rdx.

notes: Does NOT affect CF. Use add $1 if CF matters.

flags: OF SF ZF AF CF PF

description: Shift dst left by count. Fills with zeros.
Logical shift right. Fills with zeros.
Arithmetic shift right. Fills with sign bit.
Compute dst - src and set flags. Discards result.
flags: OF SF ZF AF CF PF

description: Compute dst & src and set flags. Discards result.
notes: OF and CF cleared. Use to test single bits or check for zero.

description: Jump if Greater (signed, ZF=0 and SF=OF).
Jump if Greater or Equal (signed, SF=OF).
Jump if Less or Equal (signed, ZF=1 or SF≠OF).
Jump if Above (unsigned, CF=0 and ZF=0).
Jump if Above or Equal (unsigned, CF=0).
Jump if Below or Equal (unsigned, CF=1 or ZF=1).
Push return address (%rip), then jump to target.
Pop return address from stack into %rip.

notes: Can be used for alignment or patching.

description: Fast system call. Transfers to OS. Number in %rax.
notes: Args: rdi rsi rdx r10 r8 r9. Return: rax. rcx and r11 clobbered.

description: Software interrupt. Triggers interrupt vector imm.

notes: Has implicit LOCK prefix when one operand is memory.

description: Compare %[a/r]ax with dst. If equal, dst=src. Else %ax=dst.
notes: Use with LOCK prefix for atomic CAS.

description: Byte-swap dst (reverse byte order). Used for endian conversion.
Bit Scan Forward. dst = index of lowest set bit in src.
notes: ZF set if src=0. Result undefined if src=0.

description: Bit Scan Reverse. dst = index of highest set bit in src.
Set byte to 1 if Equal (ZF=1), else 0.
notes: dst must be 8-bit reg or memory byte.

description: Repeat string operation %rcx times. Used as prefix.
Copy byte from (%rsi) to (%rdi). Advance both by 1 (direction flag).
notes: Use with REP for bulk copy.

description: Copy dword from (%rsi) to (%rdi). Advance by 4.
Copy qword from (%rsi) to (%rdi). Advance by 8.

notes: Use REP STOSB to zero/fill memory.

description: Clear Direction Flag. String ops increment %rsi/%rdi.
notes: Use before string ops unless you want decrement.

description: Set Direction Flag. String ops decrement %rsi/%rdi.

notes: cbw/cwde/cdqe are different mnemonics for the same opcode family.

description: Bus lock prefix. Makes the following RMW instruction atomic.
notes: Only valid with: add sub and or xor xchg cmpxchg inc dec neg not.

description: Read Time Stamp Counter. Result in %edx:%eax.
CPU identification. Input in %eax (leaf). Output in %eax/%ebx/%ecx/%edx.
